arguments.task: Open and read official Slack pages that support app-research fields. Visit slack.com and api.slack.com first-party pages only. Collect evidence for: one-line product/API description, documented authentication methods for Slack APIs (OAuth, tokens, Basic if any), API surface type (REST and/or GraphQL), rate limits, pricing or self-serve access for developers/apps, and whether Slack documents an MCP server. Return the exact page URLs visited and concise extracted findings with quotes or paraphrases tied to each URL. Avoid third-party sources and avoid unsupported claims.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Slack docs and pricing pages.

raw_result.description: Business messaging platform with APIs for apps and workflows to read, write, and update data in Slack.
raw_result.auth_notes: Slack documents OAuth 2.0 for app installation and access, and token-based auth for Web API calls using bot, user, or app-level tokens in the Authorization header. No first-party evidence found on the reviewed pages for HTTP Basic or API-key auth for Slack APIs.
raw_result.gating_reason: Slack provides self-serve developer access, including a free Slack Developer Program with a dedicated development sandbox for testing apps.

api_surface.breadth: Broad API surface across Slack apps and workflows, including Web API methods for reading/writing Slack data, authentication, events, and AI/MCP-related docs. Slack notes its Web API is HTTP RPC-style rather than pure REST, but it is a documented HTTP API surface suitable to classify as REST for this schema.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Slack publishes official MCP documentation, including guidance for connecting an MCP server to the Slackbot MCP Client and developing with the Slack MCP Server.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Slack documents tiered per-method, per-workspace, per-app rate limits for the Web API, including Tier 1 (1+ per minute), Tier 2 (20+ per minute), Tier 3 (50+ per minute), and Tier 4 (100+ per minute), with some special tiers and burst tolerance.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs are extensive and current; Slack explicitly documents auth, Web API behavior, rate limits, self-serve developer onboarding, and MCP-related capabilities.

result.description: Business messaging platform with APIs for apps and workflows to read, write, and update data in Slack.

result.notable_signals: Official docs are extensive and current; Slack explicitly documents auth, Web API behavior, rate limits, self-serve developer onboarding, and MCP-related capabilities.
